introduction: The most frequent painful complication of extractions. Caused by destruction of the clot that normally fills the socket

features: Pain delayed for few days up to a week after extraction
Mucosa around socket is red and tender
No clot in socket - bare whitish alveolar bone exposed

goals: Alleviate pain and suffering of the patient
Optimize condition for healing and epitheliasation of the extraction socket
Keep open socket clean and protect exposed bone
non_drug: Irrigate with mild warm saline and antiseptic
Fill with an obtundant dressing containing some non-irritant antiseptic
drug: Co-amoxiclav: 250/125 mg orally every 8 hours for 5 days (dose doubled in severe infections)
10 mL of 2% chlorhexidine gluconate solution for mouth wash three times daily

prevention: Immediately after extraction, squeeze socket edges firmly together and hold for a few minutes till clot has formed
Antibiotics if patients have had irradiation, or have Paget's disease
